title: Current Status of Foliar Diseases Resistance Breeding in Groundnut at ICRISAT Center, India
abstract:
Groundnut (Arachis hypogaea 1.) is the 6th most important oilseed crop and the 13 th
most important food crop in the world. It is grown in an area of 26.4 million ha with a total
production of 37.1 million metric tones and an average productivity of 1.4 metric tones ha-'.
Developing countries account for 96%of the global area and 92% of the global production of
the crop. Groundnut is grown under both high input and subsistence farming conditions. High
yields are realized under high input irrigated condition, where diseases and pets are controlled.
Yields in excess of 4 t ha-' are not uncommon under such conditions.............
